//: Runnable Asana example. Copy this dir into your project, set ASANA_TOKEN in a .env beside it, fill the YOUR_*/TODO_* placeholders, then `agenthook start`. See ./README.md and docs/asana-setup.md.
name: asana-example
//fullAuto: false (safe default) = agents prompt for permission. true = --dangerously-skip-permissions: a verified webhook runs UNSANDBOXED code on the host. Opt in only on a trusted host or in a container. See docs/architecture.md#security-posture.

tracker.//ids: From `agenthook init` (workspace/project typeahead + /users/me) or filled by hand.
tracker.//assigneeFilter: true (default) = act only on tasks assigned to userGid (fail-closed). false = project-wide.
tracker.//pipeline: A task ENTERING a step's sourceSectionGid fires that step. On exit the agent writes a
tracker.//pipeline2: VERDICT and the receiver moves the task: advance -> successSectionGid (= the next step's
tracker.//pipeline3: source, so the move triggers it); fail -> failureSectionGid; hold -> holdSectionGid; changes
tracker.//pipeline4: -> a prior step's source (the rework loop, capped by maxAttempts). List your section gids:

pipeline.//changes: a review `changes` verdict bounces back to the previous step (code) on the same branch/PR

pipeline.//terminal: manual = no agent runs; entry only fires system actions. drainWorktree removes the receiver-owned worktree the code/review steps shared.

ingress.//: How the receiver is reachable. ngrok (managed tunnel) | manual/hosted (static URL).
ingress.//domain: optional reserved domain for a stable URL (omit = ephemeral)
ingress.//manual: For a hosted URL instead: { type: hosted, url: https://hook.you.com }
